narrative.context: It’s exam week. Your roommate is watching reels loudly late at night. You feel irritated and your focus is slipping.
narrative.prompt: What is the most emotionally effective next step?

options.label: Pause, then assert a clear request
options.description: Take a breath, then say calmly: 'I’m struggling to focus. Can we keep audio low after 11 pm this week?'

ei.rationale: Regulates your arousal first, then uses a clear boundary to reduce conflict and protect goals—effective emotion management.

options.label: Use a practical workaround first
options.description: Put on earplugs/headphones now and plan to discuss a quiet-hours agreement tomorrow.

ei.rationale: Manages immediate distress with a coping strategy, but delays communication—still reasonably effective.

options.label: Confront sharply in the moment
options.description: Say: 'Can you stop? You’re being selfish.'

ei.rationale: Expresses emotion without regulating tone; increases defensiveness and escalates conflict.

options.label: Blast your own audio to retaliate
options.description: Play something loud so they 'get the message.'

ei.rationale: Dysregulates and amplifies conflict; avoids direct, goal-aligned management.
